+ +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask.","path":"hep/hep0.md","commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","original_commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","user":{"login":"jbednar","id":1695496,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTU0OTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1695496?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar","html_url":"https://github.com/jbednar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, are expected to be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if an enhancement is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. + +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused + +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused HEPs. + +#### Submission and Consensus-Building +Once complete, this draft should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. At this point, all members of the HoloViz community are welcome and encouraged to participate in the review in a civil and respectful manner.","path":"hep/hep0.md","commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","original_commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","user":{"login":"jbednar","id":1695496,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTU0OTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1695496?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar","html_url":"https://github.com/jbednar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion Once complete, the draft HEP should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. Identifier HEP 0
Title HEP Purpose, Policy, and Guidelines
Status Proposed
Author(s) Demetris Roumis
Created March 18, 2024
Updated March 19, 2024
Discussion https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/pull/395#issuecomment-2007702201

+ +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused HEPs. + +#### Submission and Consensus-Building +Once complete, this draft should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. At this point, all members of the HoloViz community are welcome and encouraged to participate in the review in a civil and respectful manner. + +The HEP proposer is responsible for following the discussion on the pull request, answering questions, making updates to the proposal as part of the consensus-building process, and documenting dissenting opinions. In general, the goal is to make sure that the community has consensus, not provide a rigid policy for people to try to game. When in doubt, err on the side of asking for more feedback and looking for opportunities to compromise.","path":"hep/hep0.md","commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","original_commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","user":{"login":"jbednar","id":1695496,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTU0OTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1695496?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar","html_url":"https://github.com/jbednar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion The HEP proposer is responsible for following the discussion on the pull request, answering questions, making updates to the proposal as part of the consensus-building process, and documenting dissenting opinions. Title HEP Purpose, Policy, and Guidelines

Author(s) Demetris Roumis
Created March 18, 2024

Discussion https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/pull/395#issuecomment-2007702201

+ +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused HEPs. + +#### Submission and Consensus-Building +Once complete, this draft should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. At this point, all members of the HoloViz community are welcome and encouraged to participate in the review in a civil and respectful manner. + +The HEP proposer is responsible for following the discussion on the pull request, answering questions, making updates to the proposal as part of the consensus-building process, and documenting dissenting opinions. In general, the goal is to make sure that the community has consensus, not provide a rigid policy for people to try to game. When in doubt, err on the side of asking for more feedback and looking for opportunities to compromise. + +When the proposer determines that the HEP is ready, they must notify the HoloViz core developers that it is ready for a formal review (e.g., in a comment on the pull request, using `@holoviz-dev`). This message must also describe any major points of contention and how they were resolved. + +#### Review and Resolution +A HoloViz core developer will then formally review the pull request for format, scope, and evidence of consensus. They may also notify other interested parties to weigh in on the HEP. If consensus is reached, the HoloViz core developer will update the status of the pull request, assign a HEP number, add a link to the discussion in the [HEP table](accepted_heps.md), and merge it. All HEPs will be resolved as either 'rejected', 'accepted', or 'deferred' depending on the consensus of the community.","path":"hep/hep0.md","commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","original_commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","user":{"login":"jbednar","id":1695496,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTU0OTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1695496?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar","html_url":"https://github.com/jbednar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion A HoloViz core developer will then formally review the pull request for format, scope, and evidence of consensus. + +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused HEPs. + +#### Submission and Consensus-Building +Once complete, this draft should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. At this point, all members of the HoloViz community are welcome and encouraged to participate in the review in a civil and respectful manner. + +The HEP proposer is responsible for following the discussion on the pull request, answering questions, making updates to the proposal as part of the consensus-building process, and documenting dissenting opinions. In general, the goal is to make sure that the community has consensus, not provide a rigid policy for people to try to game. When in doubt, err on the side of asking for more feedback and looking for opportunities to compromise. + +When the proposer determines that the HEP is ready, they must notify the HoloViz core developers that it is ready for a formal review (e.g., in a comment on the pull request, using `@holoviz-dev`). This message must also describe any major points of contention and how they were resolved. + +#### Review and Resolution +A HoloViz core developer will then formally review the pull request for format, scope, and evidence of consensus. They may also notify other interested parties to weigh in on the HEP. If consensus is reached, the HoloViz core developer will update the status of the pull request, assign a HEP number, add a link to the discussion in the [HEP table](accepted_heps.md), and merge it. All HEPs will be resolved as either 'rejected', 'accepted', or 'deferred' depending on the consensus of the community. + +In unusual and controversial cases, a HoloViz core developer may ask the steering committee to take a HEP up for vote, following the policies described in [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md).","path":"hep/hep0.md","commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","original_commit_id":"d647847dcb30913dddc12c9e330b9a53de9868da","user":{"login":"jbednar","id":1695496,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTU0OTY=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1695496?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar","html_url":"https://github.com/jbednar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jbednar/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion + +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused HEPs. + +#### Submission and Consensus-Building +Once complete, this draft should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. At this point, all members of the HoloViz community are welcome and encouraged to participate in the review in a civil and respectful manner. + +The HEP proposer is responsible for following the discussion on the pull request, answering questions, making updates to the proposal as part of the consensus-building process, and documenting dissenting opinions. In general, the goal is to make sure that the community has consensus, not provide a rigid policy for people to try to game. When in doubt, err on the side of asking for more feedback and looking for opportunities to compromise. + +When the proposer determines that the HEP is ready, they must notify the HoloViz core developers that it is ready for a formal review (e.g., in a comment on the pull request, using `@holoviz-dev`). This message must also describe any major points of contention and how they were resolved. + +#### Review and Resolution +A HoloViz core developer will then formally review the pull request for format, scope, and evidence of consensus. They may also notify other interested parties to weigh in on the HEP. If consensus is reached, the HoloViz core developer will update the status of the pull request, assign a HEP number, add a link to the discussion in the [HEP table](accepted_heps.md), and merge it. All HEPs will be resolved as either 'rejected', 'accepted', or 'deferred' depending on the consensus of the community. + +In unusual and controversial cases, a HoloViz core developer may ask the steering committee to take a HEP up for vote, following the policies described in [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). + +## HEP Format + +All HEPs should begin with a top-level table with the following information: + + + + + + + + + + +
Identifier [Number to be assigned by HoloViz Developer upon HEP acceptance]
Title A short title of the proposal
Status Draft | Proposed | Accepted | Rejected | Deferred | Implemented | Superseded
Author(s) Full Name [email is optional]
Created March 15, 2024
Updated March 15, 2024
Discussion link to the issue where the HEP is being discussed, NA before submission
Implementation link to the PR for the implementation, NA if not available
+ +This table should be followed by a **Summary** section and then +additional sections as needed by the proposal. + +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused HEPs. + +#### Submission and Consensus-Building +Once complete, this draft should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. At this point, all members of the HoloViz community are welcome and encouraged to participate in the review in a civil and respectful manner. + +The HEP proposer is responsible for following the discussion on the pull request, answering questions, making updates to the proposal as part of the consensus-building process, and documenting dissenting opinions. In general, the goal is to make sure that the community has consensus, not provide a rigid policy for people to try to game. When in doubt, err on the side of asking for more feedback and looking for opportunities to compromise. + +When the proposer determines that the HEP is ready, they must notify the HoloViz core developers that it is ready for a formal review (e.g., in a comment on the pull request, using `@holoviz-dev`). This message must also describe any major points of contention and how they were resolved. + +#### Review and Resolution +A HoloViz core developer will then formally review the pull request for format, scope, and evidence of consensus. They may also notify other interested parties to weigh in on the HEP. If consensus is reached, the HoloViz core developer will update the status of the pull request, assign a HEP number, add a link to the discussion in the [HEP table](accepted_heps.md), and merge it. All HEPs will be resolved as either 'rejected', 'accepted', or 'deferred' depending on the consensus of the community. + +In unusual and controversial cases, a HoloViz core developer may ask the steering committee to take a HEP up for vote, following the policies described in [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). + +## HEP Format + +All HEPs should begin with a top-level table with the following information: + + + + + + + + + + +
Identifier [Number to be assigned by HoloViz Developer upon HEP acceptance]
Title A short title of the proposal
Status Draft | Proposed | Accepted | Rejected | Deferred | Implemented | Superseded
Author(s) Full Name [email is optional]
Created March 15, 2024
Updated March 15, 2024
Discussion link to the issue where the HEP is being discussed, NA before submission
Implementation link to the PR for the implementation, NA if not available
+ +This table should be followed by a **Summary** section and then +additional sections as needed by the proposal. Some sections that may be +included if appropriate for the proposal include. + + * Specification/Policy -- The technical details of the proposed change. + * Motivation -- Why the proposed change is + +## Summary + +HEP 0 outlines the purpose, policy, and guidelines for future HEPs. This document aims to ensure that the process of proposing and implementing enhancements to the HoloViz ecosystem is clear, structured, and inclusive. + +### What is a HEP? + +A HEP is a HoloViz Enhancement Proposal, a design document that provides information to the HoloViz community or proposes a change for which widespread attention and consensus is warranted. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. HEPs should provide a concise specification and rationale for the proposed change. HEPs serve as the principal means for proposing significant changes to the HoloViz ecosystem, enabling the community to engage in informed decision-making while also documenting the proposals and the processes behind these decisions. + +HEPs are maintained as text files using Markdown for formatting in the [HoloViz/HoloViz](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) repository. Their revision history is a historical record of the proposed change. + +## Policy +### Core Developers +There are several references in this HEP to 'core developers'. This refers to the currently active HoloViz core team members, collectively described by the combined MEMBERS.md files ([example](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/project-docs/MEMBERS.md +)) in each of the HoloViz repositories. The core developers are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@holoviz-dev`. + +### Steering Committee +There are several references in this HEP to the 'steering committee'. The currently active HoloViz steering committee is in [STEERING-COMMITTEE.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/STEERING-COMMITTEE.md), and is governed by [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). The steering committee members are reachable on HoloViz [GitHub](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/rb6gPXbdAr) with the handle: `@steering-committee`. + +### Workflow +#### Initiation +The HEP process begins with an idea for a change in how HoloViz works. These changes can be technical or address the social aspects of the organization, like governance or conduct. + +Before beginning to write a HEP, it is best to ascertain if the idea fits the intent of the HEP process and has a chance of acceptance. Informally vetting an idea publicly before going as far as writing a HEP is meant to save the potential proposer time. Small or uncontroversial changes often do not need a HEP and can be decided on in the [community channels](https://holoviz.org/community.html). Major or potentially controversial changes, such as those that require collaboration across multiple HoloViz projects, should be submitted as HEPs. When it is unclear if a change is suited for a HEP, ask HoloViz core developers to provide guidance. For example, the 'new-contributors' Discord channel is open to the public and a good place to ask. + +#### Drafting +Once the proposer has confirmed with the HoloViz core developers that an idea has any chance of acceptance, a draft HEP should be written following the format described below. Drafting can take place in a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository, but the status should be changed to 'draft' while working. + +HEPs should focus on a single issue; broad changes should be split into multiple well-focused HEPs. + +#### Submission and Consensus-Building +Once complete, this draft should be submitted as a pull request to the HoloViz/HoloViz repository with the status changed to 'proposed'. At this point, all members of the HoloViz community are welcome and encouraged to participate in the review in a civil and respectful manner. + +The HEP proposer is responsible for following the discussion on the pull request, answering questions, making updates to the proposal as part of the consensus-building process, and documenting dissenting opinions. In general, the goal is to make sure that the community has consensus, not provide a rigid policy for people to try to game. When in doubt, err on the side of asking for more feedback and looking for opportunities to compromise. + +When the proposer determines that the HEP is ready, they must notify the HoloViz core developers that it is ready for a formal review (e.g., in a comment on the pull request, using `@holoviz-dev`). This message must also describe any major points of contention and how they were resolved. + +#### Review and Resolution +A HoloViz core developer will then formally review the pull request for format, scope, and evidence of consensus. They may also notify other interested parties to weigh in on the HEP. If consensus is reached, the HoloViz core developer will update the status of the pull request, assign a HEP number, add a link to the discussion in the [HEP table](accepted_heps.md), and merge it. All HEPs will be resolved as either 'rejected', 'accepted', or 'deferred' depending on the consensus of the community. + +In unusual and controversial cases, a HoloViz core developer may ask the steering committee to take a HEP up for vote, following the policies described in [CHARTER.md](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviz/blob/main/doc/governance/org-docs/CHARTER.md). + +## HEP Format + +All HEPs should begin with a top-level table with the following information: + + + + + + + + + + +
Identifier [Number to be assigned by HoloViz Developer upon HEP acceptance]
Title A short title of the proposal
Status Draft | Proposed | Accepted | Rejected | Deferred | Implemented | Superseded
Author(s) Full Name [email is optional]
Created March 15, 2024
Updated March 15, 2024
Discussion link to the issue where the HEP is being discussed, NA before submission
Implementation link to the PR for the implementation, NA if not available
+ +This table should be followed by a **Summary** section and then +additional sections as needed by the proposal. Some sections that may be +included if appropriate for the proposal include. + + * Specification/Policy -- The technical details of the proposed {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/pulls/comments/1473659833","pull_request_review_id":1855033091,"id":1473659833,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOCLF6Q85X1kO5","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,177 @@
+# Reactivity in Panel
+
+Panel's approach to expressing interactivity and managing state differs from many other frameworks and offers multiple APIs with different affordances. In this overview section we will try to convey the thinking that led to the different APIs in Panel, providing you with context on when to use which and why. In the process we will try to unpack a number of concepts that are central to most UI frameworks and how these concepts translate into Panel's unique approach.
+
+:::{note}
+Panel has gone through a significant evolution over time and many of the concepts discussed here really only fully crystallized when Param 2.0 was {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095/events","html_url":"https://github.com/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095","id":2115471316,"node_id":"I_kwDOASoy8M5-F4fU","number":6095,"title":"'color_key' argument ignored in datashade","user":{"login":"rfenouil","id":35697321,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1Njk3MzIx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35697321?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil","html_url":"https://github.com/rfenouil","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-01-09T10:16:34Z","updated_at":"2024-02-02T18:30:24Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello, thank you for the great ecosystem you are building. I'm trying to reproduce some Datashader examples and have trouble using the `color_key` parameter. It seems to be ignored and replaced internally by a default value (Set1 ?) in my attempts. I first experienced the problem with [NYC Taxi dashboard example](https://examples.holoviz.org/nyc_taxi/nyc_taxi.html), then on my own datasets. I can replicate in a minimal example below. I'm not sure if it is really a bug, related to my environment, or a misuse of the library. Any guidance on this matter would be appreciated. Thank you for your help ! #### ALL software version info ```python datashader==0.16.0 numpy==1.24.4 holoviews==1.18.1 pandas==1.5.3 dask==2023.11.0 cudf==23.12.01 dask_cudf==23.12.01 cupy==12.3.0 panel==1.3.6 param==2.0.1 colorcet==3.0.1 bokeh==3.3.2 ``` #### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior When using `datashade` with an aggregator `by("my_categories", ds.count())`, it seems that `color_key` is ignored when provided. I would expect my points to be colored according to the provided colors in `color_key` (tried with list or dict) but it seems that it uses `Set1` instead. Interestingly, the plot legend does take the specified colors as expected, but not the actual points. Additional clue : whatever the color scale specified (even colorcet with 256 colors), I cannot use more than 22 categories otherwise I get the error message: ``` ValueError('Insufficient colors provided (22) for the categorical fields available (24)') ``` First experienced that with the 24 hour coloring in [NYC Taxi dashboard example](https://examples.holoviz.org/nyc_taxi/nyc_taxi.html). That's why I suspect the `color_key` parameter is actually replaced internally with a 22 colors one. #### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue Based on [datashader documentation example](https://holoviews.org/user_guide/Large_Data.html#multidimensional-plots), here is a code example showing the problem: ```python import holoviews as hv import datashader as ds from holoviews.operation.datashader import datashade, rasterize, shade, dynspread, spread import numpy as np import pandas as pd hv.extension('bokeh','matplotlib', width=100) ### Helper functions to create points dataset # from: https://holoviews.org/user_guide/Large_Data.html#multidimensional-plots np.random.seed(3) def random_cov(): """Random covariance for use in generating 2D Gaussian distributions""" A = np.random.randn(2,2) return np.dot(A, A.T) def rand_gauss2d(value=0, n=100000): """Return a randomly shaped 2D Gaussian distribution with an associated numeric value""" g = 100*np.random.multivariate_normal(np.random.randn(2), random_cov(), (n,)) return np.hstack((g,value*np.ones((g.shape[0],1)))) ### Table of colors (as in NYC taxi dashboard example: https://examples.holoviz.org/nyc_taxi/nyc_taxi.html) colors = ["#FF0000","#FF3F00","#FF7F00","#FFBF00","#FFFF00","#BFFF00","#7FFF00","#3FFF00", "#00FF00","#00FF3F","#00FF7F","#00FFBF","#00FFFF","#00BFFF","#007FFF","#003FFF", "#0000FF","#3F00FF","#7F00FF","#BF00FF","#FF00FF","#FF00BF","#FF007F","#FF003F"] ### Generate a global dataset (5 gaussians with different values) dataset = pd.concat([pd.DataFrame(rand_gauss2d(i), columns = ["x","y","k"]) for i in range(5)]) dataset["k"] = dataset["k"].astype(str) # Convert the category to string (just to be sure) ### Create HV data representations # No "color_key" specified no_color_key = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset), aggregator=ds.by("k",ds.count())) ).opts(title="no_color_key") # "color_key" specified as list color_key_list = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset), aggregator=ds.by('k', ds.count()), color_key=colors) ).opts(title="color_key_list") # "color_key" specified as dict color_key_dict = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset), aggregator=ds.by('k', ds.count()), color_key={str(k):col for k,col in zip(dataset["k"].unique(), colors)}) # Create a dict based on 'k' names and first colors in list ).opts(title="color_key_dict") # "color_key" specified as arbitrary value color_key_arbitrary = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset), aggregator=ds.by('k', ds.count()), color_key="dummy_value") ).opts(title="color_key_arbitrary") ### Render all (no_color_key + color_key_list + color_key_dict + color_key_arbitrary).opts(width=600) ``` #### Stack traceback and/or browser JavaScript console output #### Screenshots or screencasts of the bug in action for the nicely reproducible code! I've moved this to the holoviews repository, since you're using HoloViews here and from what I can see the issue is in how HoloViews is calling Datashader rather than Datashader itself. I can reproduce the output you have above, and how I would summarize it is that the color_key is being respected for creating the legend, but somehow not being passed to Datashader for the colormapping. Maybe some of the work we did recently for supporting colormapping being done directly in Bokeh has interfered with the passing of this option down to Datashader? Definitely seems like something we need to fix. In the meantime, because of that work, you should be able to use `rasterize` instead: ```python colors = ["purple","pink","orange","springgreen","gray"] color_key_list = rasterize(hv.Points(dataset), aggregator=ds.by('k', ds.count())).opts(cmap=colors, title="color_key_list") ``` That said, using `rasterize` here is really causing my browser to have hiccups, with the correct output generated but being unresponsive for many tens of seconds. I'll open another issue about that, but in the meantime, this issue also needs fixing!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/comments/1924458790/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

#### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior
To visualize long time series data with Datashader in Dash web app.  Note that in a Jupyter notebook, the visualization works without the `rasterize()` wrapper.  And the Dash web app graph works if the X-axis is changed from datetimes to integers.  So it seems that `rasterize()` does not work with a time series axis sent through `to_dash()`.

#### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue
There is an example here:
[https://community.plotly.com/t/dash-holoviews-datashader-datetime-axes-y-u-no-work/54241](https://community.plotly.com/t/dash-holoviews-datashader-datetime-axes-y-u-no-work/54241)

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095/events","html_url":"https://github.com/holoviz/holoviews/issues/6095","id":2115471316,"node_id":"I_kwDOASoy8M5-F4fU","number":6095,"title":"'color_key' argument ignored in datashade","user":{"login":"rfenouil","id":35697321,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1Njk3MzIx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35697321?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil","html_url":"https://github.com/rfenouil","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rfenouil/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-01-09T10:16:34Z","updated_at":"2024-02-05T17:59:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello, thank you for the great ecosystem you are building.
I'm trying to reproduce some Datashader examples and have trouble using the `color_key` parameter. It seems to be ignored and replaced internally by a default value (Set1 ?) in my attempts.

I first experienced the problem with [NYC Taxi dashboard example](https://examples.holoviz.org/nyc_taxi/nyc_taxi.html), then on my own datasets.
I can replicate in a minimal example below.

I'm not sure if it is really a bug, related to my environment, or a misuse of the library.
Any guidance on this matter would be appreciated.

Thank you for your help !

#### ALL software version info

```python
datashader==0.16.0
numpy==1.24.4
holoviews==1.18.1
pandas==1.5.3
dask==2023.11.0
cudf==23.12.01
dask_cudf==23.12.01
cupy==12.3.0
panel==1.3.6
param==2.0.1
colorcet==3.0.1
bokeh==3.3.2
```

#### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior

When using `datashade` with an aggregator `by("my_categories", ds.count())`, it seems that  `color_key` is ignored when provided.
I would expect my points to be colored according to the provided colors in `color_key` (tried with list or dict) but it seems that it uses `Set1` instead.

Interestingly, the plot legend does take the specified colors as expected, but not the actual points.

Additional clue : whatever the color scale specified (even colorcet with 256 colors), I cannot use more than 22 categories otherwise I get the error message:
```
ValueError('Insufficient colors provided (22) for the categorical fields available (24)')
```

First experienced that with the 24 hour coloring in [NYC Taxi dashboard example](https://examples.holoviz.org/nyc_taxi/nyc_taxi.html).
That's why I suspect the `color_key` parameter is actually replaced internally with a 22 colors one.

#### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue

Based on [datashader documentation example](https://holoviews.org/user_guide/Large_Data.html#multidimensional-plots), here is a code example showing the problem:
```python
import holoviews as hv
import datashader as ds
from holoviews.operation.datashader import datashade, rasterize, shade, dynspread, spread
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

hv.extension('bokeh','matplotlib', width=100)

### Helper functions to create points dataset
# from: https://holoviews.org/user_guide/Large_Data.html#multidimensional-plots
np.random.seed(3)

def random_cov():
    """Random covariance for use in generating 2D Gaussian distributions"""
    A = np.random.randn(2,2)
    return np.dot(A, A.T)

def rand_gauss2d(value=0, n=100000):
    """Return a randomly shaped 2D Gaussian distribution with an associated numeric value"""
    g = 100*np.random.multivariate_normal(np.random.randn(2), random_cov(), (n,))
    return np.hstack((g,value*np.ones((g.shape[0],1))))

    
### Table of colors (as in NYC taxi dashboard example: https://examples.holoviz.org/nyc_taxi/nyc_taxi.html)
colors = ["#FF0000","#FF3F00","#FF7F00","#FFBF00","#FFFF00","#BFFF00","#7FFF00","#3FFF00",
          "#00FF00","#00FF3F","#00FF7F","#00FFBF","#00FFFF","#00BFFF","#007FFF","#003FFF",
          "#0000FF","#3F00FF","#7F00FF","#BF00FF","#FF00FF","#FF00BF","#FF007F","#FF003F"]


### Generate a global dataset (5 gaussians with different values)
dataset = pd.concat([pd.DataFrame(rand_gauss2d(i), columns = ["x","y","k"]) for i in range(5)])
dataset["k"] = dataset["k"].astype(str) # Convert the category to string (just to be sure)

### Create HV data representations
# No "color_key" specified
no_color_key = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset),
                                   aggregator=ds.by("k",ds.count()))
                        ).opts(title="no_color_key")

# "color_key" specified as list
color_key_list = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset), 
                                     aggregator=ds.by('k', ds.count()),
                                     color_key=colors)
                          ).opts(title="color_key_list")

# "color_key" specified as dict
color_key_dict = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset), 
                                     aggregator=ds.by('k', ds.count()),
                                     color_key={str(k):col for k,col in zip(dataset["k"].unique(), colors)}) # Create a dict based on 'k' names and first colors in list
                          ).opts(title="color_key_dict")

# "color_key" specified as arbitrary value
color_key_arbitrary = dynspread(datashade(hv.Points(dataset), 
                                          aggregator=ds.by('k', ds.count()),
                                          color_key="dummy_value")
                               ).opts(title="color_key_arbitrary")


### Render all
(no_color_key + color_key_list + color_key_dict + color_key_arbitrary).opts(width=600)

```

software version info

holoviews 1.18.2
datashader 0.16.0
dask 2024.1.1
bokeh 3.3.4
panel 1.3.8
python 3.9.12

Windows 11
Chrome Version 121.0.6167.140 (Official Build) (64-bit)

#### Description of expected behavior and the observed behavior

Resetting plot should get back to initial state. So the rasterization time after zooming in should be the same after the plot is displayed the first time and5after resetting the plot with the top-right tools.

#### Complete, minimal, self-contained example code that reproduces the issue

``` python
import holoviews as hv
import numpy as np
import panel as pn
from holoviews.operation.datashader import rasterize

hv.extension("bokeh")


def plot_ts(timeseries: list):
    # generate curve for each ts
    curve = None
    for ts in timeseries:
        if curve is None:
            curve = rasterize(
                hv.Curve(
                    (
                        np.arange(len(ts)),
                        ts,
                    ),
                )
            )
        else:
            curve += rasterize(
                hv.Curve(
                    (
                        np.arange(len(ts)),
                        ts,
                    ),
                )
            )
    return curve


if __name__ == "__main__":
    timeseries = [np.random.rand(50000) for _ in range(20)]
    curves = plot_ts(timeseries)
    curves.cols(4)
    panel = pn.panel(curves)
    pn.serve(panel)

```

#### Screenshots or screencasts of the bug in action
![holoviews](https://github.com/holoviz/holoviews/assets/7569375/59515e2c-48df-46e3-ac7b-603e0d52289c)

It is worth mentioning that when plotting only 12 timeseries (instead of 20 here), the delay decreases drastically.

I'd be happy to help if I can ! 3.12","user":{"login":"maximlt","id":35924738,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTI0NzM4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35924738?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt","html_url":"https://github.com/maximlt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Colorcet is a simple package, its dev tooling and infra can be simplified. Principles adopted:

- Keeps things very simple
- Use very standard tools

As a consequence:

- conda is only used when the conda package is built and pushed to pyviz, otherwise it's pip
- no more pyctdev, simple commands are called directly
- no more custom versioning, use `setuptools_scm` instead
- the site is no longer built directly with nbsite but with sphinx instead
- pyproject.toml only instead of setup.py + setup.cfg
- dropped 3.7 as for some reason `pip install -e .` wouldn't work on this version, and added 3.12 since it was easy.

I **removed** the `colorcet examples` CLI as it was one less thing to migrate moving from setup.py to pyproject.toml. I guess I could add it again if we want to keep it, although it's a bit annoying as I also moved all the notebook files from /examples to /doc to build with Sphinx directly. Note that Panel removed `panel examples` is version 1.0.

I thought about moving to hatch/hatchling too in this PR but stuck to setuptools for now, it made my life easier. But from this point on it should be pretty easy to convert to hatch/hatchling if need be.

A change I noticed is that now the sdist has all the tracked files in the repository while it contained less files. This is because `setuptools_scm` provide a list of all tracked files to `setuptools`. It could be reduced by pruning dirs and files in `MANIFEST.in`. I don't see a need to do that.

EDIT:

Happy to report that all the three Github workflows are working with this setup. Also, for some reason I had to update a couple of base images in https://github.com/holoviz/colorcet/pull/120/commits/012124efc27dbeb2a0e42790f87e2f70085182ba. The diff shows that it had something to do with the font more than with the palette.

dev tooling, remove `colorcet examples`, drop 3.7, add 3.12","user":{"login":"maximlt","id":35924738,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTI0NzM4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35924738?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt","html_url":"https://github.com/maximlt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Colorcet is a simple package, its dev tooling and infra can be simplified. Principles adopted:

- Keeps things very simple
- Use very standard tools

As a consequence:

- conda is only used when the conda package is built and pushed to pyviz, otherwise it's pip
- no more pyctdev, simple commands are called directly
- no more custom versioning, use `setuptools_scm` instead
- the site is no longer built directly with nbsite but with sphinx instead
- pyproject.toml only instead of setup.py + setup.cfg
- dropped 3.7 as for some reason `pip install -e .` wouldn't work on this version, and added 3.12 since it was easy.

I **removed** the `colorcet examples` CLI as it was one less thing to migrate moving from setup.py to pyproject.toml. I guess I could add it again if we want to keep it, although it's a bit annoying as I also moved all the notebook files from /examples to /doc to build with Sphinx directly. Note that Panel removed `panel examples` is version 1.0.

I thought about moving to hatch/hatchling too in this PR but stuck to setuptools for now, it made my life easier. But from this point on it should be pretty easy to convert to hatch/hatchling if need be.

A change I noticed is that now the sdist has all the tracked files in the repository while it contained less files. This is because `setuptools_scm` provide a list of all tracked files to `setuptools`. It could be reduced by pruning dirs and files in `MANIFEST.in`. I don't see a need to do that.

EDIT:

Happy to report that all the three Github workflows are working with this setup. Also, for some reason I had to update a couple of base images in https://github.com/holoviz/colorcet/pull/120/commits/012124efc27dbeb2a0e42790f87e2f70085182ba. The diff shows that it had something to do with the font more than with the palette.

3.12","user":{"login":"maximlt","id":35924738,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTI0NzM4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35924738?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt","html_url":"https://github.com/maximlt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/maximlt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Colorcet is a simple package, its dev tooling and infra can be simplified. Principles adopted:

- Keeps things very simple
- Use very standard tools

As a consequence:

- conda is only used when the conda package is built and pushed to pyviz, otherwise it's pip
- no more pyctdev, simple commands are called directly
- no more custom versioning, use `setuptools_scm` instead
- the site is no longer built directly with nbsite but with sphinx instead
- pyproject.toml only instead of setup.py + setup.cfg
- dropped 3.7 as for some reason `pip install -e .` wouldn't work on this version, and added 3.12 since it was easy.

I **removed** the `colorcet examples` CLI as it was one less thing to migrate moving from setup.py to pyproject.toml. I guess I could add it again if we want to keep it, although it's a bit annoying as I also moved all the notebook files from /examples to /doc to build with Sphinx directly. Note that Panel removed `panel examples` is version 1.0.

I thought about moving to hatch/hatchling too in this PR but stuck to setuptools for now, it made my life easier. But from this point on it should be pretty easy to convert to hatch/hatchling if need be.

A change I noticed is that now the sdist has all the tracked files in the repository while it contained less files. This is because `setuptools_scm` provide a list of all tracked files to `setuptools`. It could be reduced by pruning dirs and files in `MANIFEST.in`. I don't see a need to do that.

EDIT:

Happy to report that all the three Github workflows are working with this setup. Also, for some reason I had to update a couple of base images in https://github.com/holoviz/colorcet/pull/120/commits/012124efc27dbeb2a0e42790f87e2f70085182ba. The diff shows that it had something to do with the font more than with the palette.
